CHANGE RECORD 
 
 
 
When the quality of the products changes, the QuID is updated and a row is 
added to this table. The third column specifies which sections or sub-sections 
have been updated.  The fourth column should mention the version of the  
product to which the change applies.

I INTRODUCTION 
 
 
 
 
I.1 Product name 
 
 
OMI_CLIMATE_sst_BAL_area_averaged_anomalies and OMI_CLIMATE_sst_BAL_trend 
 
 
I.2 Indicator evaluation method 
 
 
All anomalies have been calculated with respect to a monthly climatology from 
1991-2020. To calculate the trends in the SST time series and the confidence 
intervals on the trends, we used the Mann -Kendall Tau -b non-parametric 
function with Sens method to compute a coefficient representing a trend for  
equally spaced data (Mann 1945; Sen 1968; Kendall 1975). A 95% confidence  
interval for the trend estimate is chosen here. 
The SST Level 4 analysis product that provides the input to the monthly averages is
 taken from the reprocessed dataset 
SST_BAL_SST_L4_REP_OBSERVATIONS_010_016 with the regular updates based  upon C3S 
SST observations. The climatology period from 1991 to 2020 (30 years) is selected 
according to WMO recommendations (WMO, 2017) and the most recent 
practice from the U.S. National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration 
practice (https://wmo.int/media/news/updated -30-year-reference-period-
reflects-changing-climate). 
 
I.3 CMEMS products used as input for the computations
